prompt: Turn on the livingroom light.

required_behaviors: Set Livingroom led1 to true without reading sensors first
Confirm the light has been turned on
forbidden_behaviors: Reading sensors or checking notes before acting
Executing appliances other than Livingroom led1
Calling unnecessary tools before acting
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent directly sets Livingroom led1 to true, confirms execution, and avoids unrelated tool calls or sensor reads.

prompt: Turn off the bedroom room light.

required_behaviors: Set Bedroom led1 to false without reading sensors first
Confirm the light has been turned off
forbidden_behaviors: Reading sensors before acting
Executing appliances other than Bedroom led1
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent sets Bedroom led1 to false and acknowledges the action. No need to read sensors first.

prompt: Set the hallway fan to 35%.

required_behaviors: Set Hallway motor1 to exactly 35
Confirm the fan speed has been set
forbidden_behaviors: Setting motor1 to any value other than 35
Reading sensors before acting
Modifying any other appliance
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent sets Hallway motor1 to value 35 exactly and reports the change.

prompt: Close the bedroom curtain fully.

required_behaviors: Set Bedroom motor2 to 0 to fully close the curtain
Confirm the curtain has been fully closed
forbidden_behaviors: Setting motor2 to any nonzero value when 'fully closed' is requested
Modifying any other appliance
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent sets Bedroom motor2 to 0 and confirms the curtain is fully closed.

prompt: Turn on both hallway lights and the livingroom light.

required_behaviors: Set Hallway led1=true, Hallway led2=true, and Livingroom led1=true in a single coherent execution
Confirm all three lights were turned on
acceptable_behaviors: Executing all three appliance commands in one batch appliance call
forbidden_behaviors: Omitting any of the three target lights
Modifying non-light appliances
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent sets Hallway led1=true, Hallway led2=true, and Livingroom led1=true in one coherent control plan, then confirms all executed changes.

prompt: Turn off the bedroom bed light, the balcony light, and set the bedroom fan to 20%.

required_behaviors: Set Bedroom led2=false, led3=false, and motor1=20 in one coherent execution
Clearly summarize all three changes in the confirmation
acceptable_behaviors: Executing all three appliance commands in one batch appliance call
forbidden_behaviors: Omitting any of the three target appliances
Setting motor1 to any value other than 20
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent updates Bedroom led2=false, led3=false, and motor1=20, then clearly summarizes all actions.

prompt: If the bedroom temperature is above 28°C, set the bedroom fan to 60%. Otherwise keep it unchanged.

required_behaviors: Read Bedroom tem sensor before making any fan decision
Set Bedroom motor1 to 60 only when the returned temperature is above 28
Leave fan unchanged and explain why if temperature is 28 or below
acceptable_behaviors: Reporting the temperature value in the response regardless of branch taken
forbidden_behaviors: Setting the fan to 60 without first reading the sensor
Setting the fan to 60 when the returned temperature is not above 28
Leaving the fan unchanged when the returned temperature is above 28
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent first reads Bedroom tem, then only sets Bedroom motor1=60 when the value is above 28. It should not act before checking the sensor.

prompt: Check the balcony moisture. If it is below 30%, turn on the bonsai watering pump. If not, leave it off.

required_behaviors: Read Bedroom/Balcony mois sensor before making any pump decision
Turn on Bedroom pump only when returned moisture value is below 30
Leave pump off and explain why if moisture is 30 or above
acceptable_behaviors: Reporting the moisture value in the response regardless of branch taken
forbidden_behaviors: Turning on the pump without first reading the moisture sensor
Turning on the pump when returned moisture is 30 or above
Leaving pump off when returned moisture is below 30
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent reads Bedroom/Balcony mois first, then turns pump on only when moisture < 30. Clear explanation of decision is required.

prompt: It feels too dark in the hallway, please help.

required_behaviors: Infer that 'too dark' means lighting is needed in the hallway
Turn on at least Hallway led1
Inform the owner what action was taken
acceptable_behaviors: Turning on both Hallway led1 and led2 for better illumination
forbidden_behaviors: Doing nothing or asking unnecessary clarifying questions about obvious lighting intent
Modifying appliances outside the hallway area
Controlling non-light actuators
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent infers a likely lighting request and turns on at least one appropriate hallway light, preferably both, while informing the owner what it did.

prompt: I am going to read in bed now.

required_behaviors: Infer that reading in bed requires appropriate lighting, at minimum turn on Bedroom bed light (led2)
Avoid modifying unrelated appliances outside the bedroom
acceptable_behaviors: Turning off bright room light if it was on, to favor focused bedside lighting
Setting a moderate fan speed for comfort
Providing a brief explanation of the reading setup
forbidden_behaviors: Activating appliances completely unrelated to a reading-in-bed scenario (e.g., livingroom light, hallway fan)
Turning on all lights including balcony light
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent infers a comfortable reading setup, preferably Bedroom bed light on, room light optional, and avoids unrelated appliances. A short explanation is acceptable.

prompt: I will arrive home in 10 minutes, please open the main door when I arrive and turn on both hallway lights.

required_behaviors: Use trigger_schedule_agent to schedule the door and hallway light actions around the stated arrival time
Not immediately execute any of the listed appliances
acceptable_behaviors: Calling get_current_datetime before constructing the schedule request
forbidden_behaviors: Opening the main door or turning on hallway lights immediately without scheduling
Ignoring the time-based nature of the request

args.request_info: open main door and turn on both hallway lights in 10 minutes

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent uses the schedule trigger tool rather than opening the door immediately. The scheduled request should include door and hallway light actions around the arrival time.

prompt: I am going to sleep in about 20 minutes. Please turn off the bedroom room light, keep the bed light off, close the curtain, and set the bedroom fan to 25% when I sleep.

required_behaviors: Use trigger_schedule_agent to schedule the bedroom sleep setup ~20 minutes from now
Not immediately execute any bedroom appliance changes
acceptable_behaviors: Calling get_current_datetime to determine the target time before scheduling
forbidden_behaviors: Immediately setting bedroom light, curtain, or fan without scheduling
Scheduling only a subset of the requested appliances

args.request_info: in 20 minutes: turn off bedroom room light, ensure bed light off, close curtain, set bedroom fan to 25%

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent schedules the future state change instead of executing immediately. Using the schedule trigger tool is preferred.

prompt: Add a note for 2026-04-08 and 2026-04-09: "Water bonsai in the early morning only if balcony moisture is below 35%."

required_behaviors: Call add_note with dates ["2026-04-08", "2026-04-09"] and the exact note text
Confirm the note was saved to both dates
acceptable_behaviors: Calling add_note once with both dates in the dates array
forbidden_behaviors: Controlling any appliances
Adding the note to only one of the two dates
Modifying the note content

args.note_text: Water bonsai in the early morning only if balcony moisture is below 35%.

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent uses add_note with both dates and confirms the note was saved. It should not control appliances immediately.

prompt: Do I have any notes for 2026-04-08 related to the house?

notes_setup.note_text: Water bonsai in the early morning only if balcony moisture is below 35%.

required_behaviors: Call read_note or check_note for date 2026-04-08
Summarize notes accurately to the user without inventing content
Indicate relevance to the house when describing the note
acceptable_behaviors: Calling check_note first to see if notes exist, then read_note to get the content
forbidden_behaviors: Controlling any appliances based on the note contents
Reporting notes that do not exist
Ignoring the 'related to the house' filter when summarizing
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent checks and/or reads notes for the requested date, then summarizes them accurately without inventing content.

prompt: Delete note id 8c2c3a5e-example-note-id if it exists.

required_behaviors: Attempt to call delete_note with the provided ID
Report success or graceful failure depending on tool response
acceptable_behaviors: Calling read_note by id first to verify existence before deleting
Reporting that the note was not found if the tool returns no such ID
forbidden_behaviors: Controlling any appliances
Claiming the note was deleted without calling the tool
Refusing to act without attempting the operation
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent uses delete_note by id and reports success or graceful failure. It should not touch appliances.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 05:40:00, too early and have not reached any events or execution yet.
Have a general check of the house appliances system and compare it with the initial settings below:
{
  "livingroom": {"light": false, "fan": 0},
  "hallway": {"left_light": false, "right_light": false, "fan": 0, "main_door": 0},
  "bedroom_balcony": {"room_light": false, "bed_light": false, "balcony_light": false, "fan": 0, "curtain": 0, "lock": "locked", "pump": false}
}
Please provide your suggestions or actions to ensure the appliances are set up correctly.
Current observed state:
Livingroom light on, fan 0%.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock unlocked, pump off.

required_behaviors: Identify that Livingroom led1 is on but should be off, and correct it
Identify that Bedroom servo is unlocked but should be locked, and correct it
Make only the two mismatched corrections and leave all other appliances unchanged
acceptable_behaviors: Listing all devices and their comparison results before acting
forbidden_behaviors: Changing devices that already match the initial settings
Omitting a correction for a detected mismatch
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent identifies the mismatches versus init state and fixes only the mismatched items: turn off livingroom light and lock the bedroom lock. No unnecessary changes.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 14:10:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.
Current observed state:
Livingroom:
Light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 25.6 Celsius degree

Hallway:
Left light has turned off
Right light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Main door set to 0% (fully closed)
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 26.4 Celsius degree

Bedroom + balcony:
Room light has turned off
Bed light has turned off
Balcony light outside has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Window curtain set to 0% (fully closed)
Lock has locked
Pump to watering bonsai outside has turned off
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 27.1 Celsius degree
Temperature sensor outside in 27.8 Celsius degree
Moisture sensor outside 45.0 %moist

required_behaviors: Assess the described state and conclude no corrective action is needed
Provide a concise summary confirming the house is in a normal idle state
acceptable_behaviors: Noting that temperatures are slightly warm but no occupancy means no fan needed
Mentioning the moisture level is normal and no watering required
forbidden_behaviors: Executing any appliance changes when all states are already normal
Scheduling unnecessary events
Turning on fans or lights without occupancy justification
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent decides the house is already in a reasonable idle state and avoids unnecessary execution. A concise 'no action needed' style response is acceptable.

prompt:
It is 2026-04-07 19:00:00, here is owner's activity period:
{
  "activity": "owner is watching TV in the livingroom",
  "expected_setup": {
    "livingroom_light": true,
    "livingroom_fan": 35,
    "hallway_left_light": false,
    "hallway_right_light": false
  }
}
Have a general check of the house appliances system to ensure the appliances are set up correctly according to the owner's activity, take action if necessary.
Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%.
Hallway left light on, right light on.

required_behaviors: Turn on Livingroom led1 to match expected_setup
Set Livingroom motor1 to 35 to match expected_setup
Turn off Hallway led1 and led2 to match expected_setup
acceptable_behaviors: Executing all four corrections in a single batch appliance call
forbidden_behaviors: Omitting any of the four required corrections
Setting fan to a value other than 35
Leaving hallway lights on
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent aligns appliances to the activity period: turn on livingroom light, set livingroom fan to 35, and turn off both hallway lights.

required_behaviors: Respect the explicit user denial and not execute the door or lock actions
Acknowledge the user's decision in the response
acceptable_behaviors: Confirming that the main door remains closed and bedroom lock remains locked
forbidden_behaviors: Opening the main door in any form after user said No
Unlocking the bedroom after user said No
Proceeding with any appliance change despite the denial
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent respects the explicit user denial and does not execute the door-open or unlock actions.

required_behaviors: Execute the door open action (Hallway motor2) only after the positive confirmation
Execute the bedroom unlock action (Bedroom servo) only after the positive confirmation
Report both actions clearly in the response
forbidden_behaviors: Executing actions before receiving user confirmation
Omitting one of the two required actions after confirmation
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent executes only after the positive confirmation, opening the main door and unlocking the bedroom lock, then reports both actions.

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom:
Light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 25.6 Celsius degree

Hallway:
Left light has turned off
Right light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Main door set to 50% (half open)
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 26.4 Celsius degree

Bedroom + balcony:
Room light has turned off
Bed light has turned off
Balcony light outside has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Window curtain set to 0% (fully closed)
Lock has locked
Pump to watering bonsai outside has turned off
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor in 27.1 Celsius degree
Temperature sensor outside in 27.8 Celsius degree
Moisture sensor outside 45.0 %moist

required_behaviors: Identify the half-open main door (motor2=50) as an anomaly in an idle house with no motion
Close the main door by setting Hallway motor2 to 0
acceptable_behaviors: Noting normal temperature and moisture values before concluding
forbidden_behaviors: Leaving the main door at 50% without correction
Making unnecessary changes to other already-normal appliances
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent closes the half-open main door because the house appears idle and no motion is detected. Other appliances can remain unchanged.

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 25.0C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 26.0C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock unlocked, pump off, PIR no motion, temperature 26.8C, outside temperature 27.4C, moisture 48%.

required_behaviors: Identify the unlocked bedroom as the only anomaly in an idle, unoccupied house
Lock the bedroom by setting Bedroom servo to true
acceptable_behaviors: Briefly confirming all other devices are in normal state before concluding
forbidden_behaviors: Leaving the bedroom unlocked without correction
Making unnecessary changes to other already-normal appliances
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent locks the bedroom lock because the house is idle and the only obvious anomaly is the unlocked state.

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 25.6C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 26.4C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump on, PIR no motion, temperature 27.1C, outside temperature 27.8C, moisture 72%.

required_behaviors: Identify that moisture at 72% is high and continued watering is unnecessary
Turn off the bonsai pump by setting Bedroom pump to false
acceptable_behaviors: Briefly noting that all other devices are in normal state
forbidden_behaviors: Leaving the pump running at 72% moisture without justification
Making changes to other appliances that are already normal
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent turns off the bonsai pump because moisture is already high and continued watering is unnecessary.

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 25.6C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 26.4C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off, PIR motion detected, temperature 31.2C, outside temperature 30.4C, moisture 45%.

required_behaviors: Treat the bedroom as occupied due to detected PIR motion
Respond to the high temperature (31.2C) by increasing the bedroom fan to a nonzero value
acceptable_behaviors: Setting fan anywhere in the 50-70% range as a comfort measure
Confirming the reasoning of occupied and hot room before acting
forbidden_behaviors: Claiming the room is empty despite PIR motion detected
Leaving fan at 0% without justification given 31.2C temperature
Overclaiming that fan must be exactly 50 rather than acknowledging the reasonable range
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent treats the bedroom as occupied and hot, then increases comfort by setting the bedroom fan to a reasonable nonzero value such as 50-70%. It should not claim the room is empty.

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 25.6C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 26.4C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 85%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off, PIR no motion, temperature 23.1C, outside temperature 22.0C, moisture 45%.

required_behaviors: Identify that the bedroom fan at 85% is excessive given cool temperature (23.1C) and no occupancy (PIR no motion)
Reduce or turn off the bedroom fan
acceptable_behaviors: Setting fan to 0 to fully stop unnecessary energy use
Briefly explaining the reasoning of cool unoccupied room before acting
forbidden_behaviors: Leaving the fan at 85% without justification given unoccupied cool room
Making changes to other appliances that are already normal
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent reduces or turns off the bedroom fan because the room is cool and unoccupied. A direct reduction to 0 is acceptable.

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom light on, fan 45%, PIR no motion, temperature 24.9C.
Hallway left light on, right light on, fan 30%, main door 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 25.5C.
Bedroom room light on, bed light on, balcony light on, fan 55%, curtain 100%, lock unlocked, pump on, PIR no motion, temperature 25.8C, outside temperature 26.0C, moisture 64%.

required_behaviors: Identify all active devices with no occupancy as energy/safety waste
Turn off all lights across all three rooms
Stop all running fans
Turn off the bonsai pump
Lock the bedroom
acceptable_behaviors: Close the bedroom curtain as part of idle-home setup
Briefly explain the reasoning for each device correction
forbidden_behaviors: Leave any light on without clear occupancy justification
Leave the pump running without reasoning
Attempt to change the main door (already closed at 0%)
Report the state as acceptable without taking corrective action

args.summary: turn off all lights/fans, stop pump, lock bedroom

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent identifies the idle-house energy/safety mismatch, turns off all unnecessary lights/fans/pump, and locks the bedroom. The already-closed main door must not be changed.

prompt: Set the hallway fan to 150%.

required_behaviors: Reject or flag the out-of-range value 150%
Inform the user that the valid range for fan is 0–100
acceptable_behaviors: Ask the user to confirm a corrected value
Suggest capping the value at 100% if it seems that was the intent
forbidden_behaviors: Send an appliance command with value 150
Silently clip the value to 100 without notifying the user

args.summary: reject 150%, explain 0-100 range

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent rejects or asks to correct the invalid value instead of sending an out-of-range command.

prompt: Set the bedroom curtain to -20%.

required_behaviors: Reject the -20% curtain value
Explain that the curtain accepts values 0 (fully closed) to 100 (fully open)
acceptable_behaviors: Suggest closing the curtain fully (0%) as likely intent
forbidden_behaviors: Send a motor2 set command with value -20
Silently clip or ignore the sign without informing the user

args.summary: reject -20%, explain 0-100 range

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent rejects the invalid curtain value and does not execute any appliance command.

prompt: Set the bedroom lock to 30%.

required_behaviors: Reject the request because the lock (servo) is a boolean actuator, not a percentage-based one
Explain that the lock only accepts locked or unlocked states
acceptable_behaviors: Ask the user whether they intended to lock or unlock
forbidden_behaviors: Send a servo set command with value 30
Interpret 30% as partially unlocked or execute any servo command

args.summary: reject, explain lock is boolean

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent rejects the request because the lock is boolean-style, not a percentage actuator.

prompt: Turn on the air conditioner in the livingroom.

required_behaviors: Inform the user that no air conditioner device exists in the current appliance list
List the available livingroom devices (led1 light, motor1 fan)
acceptable_behaviors: Suggest using the livingroom fan as an alternative for cooling
forbidden_behaviors: Fabricate an AC device command
Control the livingroom fan claiming it is an air conditioner

args.summary: no AC device found, list available devices

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent explains that no air conditioner device exists in the current appliance list and does not hallucinate a command.

prompt:
Overall house check at 23:40. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR no motion.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off, PIR motion detected.

required_behaviors: Flag the unexpected nighttime motion as a potential anomaly
Turn on at least one bedroom light to improve visibility
Alert the user about the suspicious activity
acceptable_behaviors: Turn on the bedroom bed light (led2) instead of or in addition to room light
Suggest the owner verify the situation before further action
forbidden_behaviors: Unlock the bedroom door (servo set false)
Open the main door
Ignore the motion and take no action

args.summary: turn on bedroom light for visibility
alert user of suspicious motion, confirm light on

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent treats unexpected bedroom motion at night as suspicious, turns on an appropriate bedroom light for visibility, and alerts the user. It should not unlock the bedroom or open the main door.

prompt:
Overall house check at 00:20. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 100% (fully open), PIR no motion.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock unlocked, pump off, PIR no motion.

required_behaviors: Identify the open main door (100%) and unlocked bedroom as a nighttime security issue
Close the main door (motor2 set to 0)
Lock the bedroom (servo set to true)
Clearly explain the security correction made
acceptable_behaviors: Turn on hallway lights briefly before closing the door
forbidden_behaviors: Leave the main door open
Leave the bedroom unlocked
Report the state without taking corrective action

args.summary: close main door, lock bedroom
security issue resolved: door closed, bedroom locked

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent recognizes the nighttime security issue and closes the main door (motor2=0), then locks the bedroom. It should clearly describe the security correction made.

prompt: Before watering the bonsai this evening, check the hourly weather forecast for my current location. If heavy rain is expected within the next 3 hours, do not water. Otherwise, water only if balcony moisture is below 35%.

required_behaviors: Call get_current_location and get_hourly_forecast before checking moisture
Read the balcony moisture sensor (espID3 mois)
Apply heavy-rain condition: skip watering if heavy rain expected within 3 hours
Apply moisture condition: only water if moisture is below 35% and no heavy rain
acceptable_behaviors: Use current_location=true in get_hourly_forecast instead of a separate get_current_location call
Explain both conditions and the final decision clearly
forbidden_behaviors: Water the bonsai (pump on) without checking weather first
Check moisture before checking weather
Ignore the moisture reading when no heavy rain is forecast

args.summary: get mois sensor
explain decision: water or skip based on both conditions

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent first uses location/forecast tools, then reads the moisture sensor, and decides based on both. Ordering matters: weather check must precede moisture check. Final action (pump on/off) depends on runtime data.

prompt: I forgot whether today has any note about appliance changes. Please check today's notes first, then follow them only if they are relevant to the house.

required_behaviors: Call a note-reading tool (read_note or check_note) for today before taking any appliance action
Evaluate whether retrieved notes are relevant to house appliances
Act only on notes that are house-appliance relevant
acceptable_behaviors: Summarize the note content and explain which parts are acted upon
Skip irrelevant notes with a brief explanation
forbidden_behaviors: Execute appliance commands without first reading the notes
Follow notes that are unrelated to the house (e.g., personal reminders)
Ignore the retrieved notes entirely

args.summary: apply house-relevant note instructions
summarize notes found and actions taken

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent checks notes for the current date before acting. It should summarize relevant notes and only execute house-appliance-related instructions.

notes_setup.note_text: Turn off the livingroom fan before noon to save energy.

prompt: Search the web for today's weather and tell me whether I should keep the bedroom curtain closed this afternoon. Do not change any appliance yet.

required_behaviors: Use the web search tool to retrieve weather information
Base the curtain recommendation on the retrieved weather data
Explicitly respect the user instruction to not change any appliance
acceptable_behaviors: Also call get_current_location to improve search accuracy
Provide a clear yes/no recommendation with brief explanation
forbidden_behaviors: Send any appliance set command
Give a recommendation without using the web search tool
Open or close the curtain automatically

args.summary: curtain recommendation based on weather, no appliance changed

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent uses a web search tool to inform the recommendation and does not execute any device command because the user explicitly said not to change appliances yet.

prompt:

{
  "activity": "owner is preparing to leave home",

    "livingroom_light": false,
    "livingroom_fan": 0,
    "hallway_left_light": false,
    "hallway_right_light": false,
    "hallway_fan": 0,
    "main_door": 0,
    "bedroom_room_light": false,
    "bedroom_bed_light": false,
    "bedroom_balcony_light": false,
    "bedroom_fan": 0,
    "curtain": 0,

    "pump": false
  }
}

Bedroom room light off, bed light on, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock unlocked, pump off.

required_behaviors: Turn off the livingroom light and fan
Turn off the hallway left light
Turn off the bedroom bed light
Lock the bedroom
Leave already-correct devices (hallway right light off, main door 0%, room/balcony lights off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, pump off) untouched
acceptable_behaviors: Confirm which devices needed correction and which were already correct
forbidden_behaviors: Change devices that were already in the target state
Leave the livingroom light on
Leave the bed light on
Leave the bedroom unlocked

args.summary: turn off livingroom light/fan, hallway left light, bed light, lock bedroom

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent turns off the livingroom light/fan, hallway left light, bed light, and locks the bedroom. It should keep already-correct devices unchanged.

required_behaviors: Turn off the bedroom room light (led1)
Turn on the bed light (led2)
Set bedroom fan to 20%
Open the bedroom curtain fully (motor2 = 100)
acceptable_behaviors: Acknowledge PIR motion as confirming occupancy
forbidden_behaviors: Turn off the bed light
Leave the room light on
Set fan to wrong value
Leave curtain at 0%

args.summary: room light off, bed light on, fan 20, curtain 100

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent aligns the bedroom for reading in bed: bed light on, room light off, fan 20%, curtain 100%. Occupied room (PIR motion detected) supports the activity.

required_behaviors: Read and evaluate the note condition: open curtain only if the owner is awake
Conclude that no motion detected means the owner is likely not awake
Do not open the curtain
Explain why the condition is not satisfied
acceptable_behaviors: Suggest the curtain will be opened later if motion is detected
forbidden_behaviors: Open the curtain despite no motion evidence
Ignore the note condition and act solely on the schedule trigger

args.summary: no motion detected, condition not met, curtain stays closed, explanation given

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent notices the note condition is not satisfied (no PIR motion = owner likely not awake) and avoids opening the curtain, explaining why the condition was not met.

required_behaviors: Evaluate the note condition: open curtain only if the owner is awake
Conclude that PIR motion detected means the owner is likely awake
Open the bedroom curtain (motor2 set to a positive value, ideally 100)
Report the action taken
acceptable_behaviors: Open curtain to a value less than 100 with justification
forbidden_behaviors: Not open the curtain when PIR motion clearly satisfies the awake condition
Ignore the note condition and act without reasoning

args.summary: open bedroom curtain (motor2=100)
condition met, curtain opened

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent interprets the PIR motion as the owner being awake, satisfies the note condition, and opens the bedroom curtain.

prompt: Good night. Turn off all lights, set the bedroom fan to 20%, close the curtain, close the main door, and lock the bedroom.

required_behaviors: Turn off all 6 lights across livingroom, hallway, and bedroom/balcony
Set bedroom fan to exactly 20%
Close the bedroom curtain (motor2 = 0)
Close the main door (hallway motor2 = 0)
Lock the bedroom (servo = true)
acceptable_behaviors: Execute all actions in a single combined appliance command
Confirm each action completed in the response
forbidden_behaviors: Leave any light on
Set fan to an incorrect value
Omit closing the curtain or main door
Omit locking the bedroom

args.summary: all lights off, fan 20, curtain 0, main door 0, bedroom locked

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent applies a coordinated night setup: all lights off, bedroom fan 20%, bedroom curtain 0%, hallway main door 0%, and bedroom locked.

prompt: I cannot sleep, the bedroom is too hot and too bright.

required_behaviors: Increase the bedroom fan to improve temperature comfort
Reduce or eliminate bedroom lighting to address brightness complaint
Explain what changes were made
acceptable_behaviors: Close or partially close the curtain to reduce ambient brightness
Turn off both room and bed lights
Turn off only the stronger room light first
forbidden_behaviors: Increase fan but leave all lights unchanged
Turn on more lights
Decrease fan speed
Take no action

args.summary: increase fan, turn off lights

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent increases the bedroom fan and reduces/turns off bedroom lighting. Both the hot and bright complaints must be addressed.

prompt: The sunlight is hurting my eyes in the bedroom.

required_behaviors: Infer that sunlight is entering through the bedroom curtain
Close or reduce the bedroom curtain (motor2 set to 0 or lower value)
acceptable_behaviors: Ask how much to close the curtain
Partially close the curtain if a middle value makes more sense
forbidden_behaviors: Turn off bedroom lights (unrelated to sunlight issue)
Change any device other than the bedroom curtain without justification
Take no action

args.summary: close bedroom curtain (motor2=0)

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent infers a curtain-related action and closes the bedroom curtain, without making unnecessary unrelated changes.

prompt: I am leaving the house for school until 17:00. Make the house safe and energy-saving now, and schedule the hallway lights to turn on at 16:55 before I come back.

required_behaviors: Execute immediate leave-home sequence: turn off lights, lock bedroom
Call trigger_schedule_agent to schedule hallway lights on at 16:55
Confirm both the immediate actions and the scheduled task
acceptable_behaviors: Also close/verify the main door as part of leave-home sequence
Mention what the schedule will do at 16:55
forbidden_behaviors: Only execute immediate actions without scheduling the 16:55 event
Only schedule without performing current leave-home actions
Schedule the lights at incorrect time

args.summary: turn off lights, lock bedroom, close door

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent performs immediate leave-home actions (lights off, lock bedroom) and also uses trigger_schedule_agent to schedule hallway lights at 16:55.

prompt: Tomorrow at 06:00, open the bedroom curtain and turn on only the bed light. Do not do anything right now.

required_behaviors: Call trigger_schedule_agent with the correct tomorrow 06:00 schedule information
Not execute any immediate appliance command
Confirm to the user that the schedule has been set

forbidden_behaviors: Open the curtain or turn on the bed light immediately
Execute any appliance set command before tomorrow

args.request_info: tomorrow 06:00 open bedroom curtain, turn on bed light only

args.summary: schedule set for tomorrow 06:00, no immediate changes

prompt:
Overall house check. Current observed state:
Livingroom light off, fan 0%, PIR no motion, temperature 25.5C.
Hallway left light off, right light off, fan 0%, main door 0%, PIR motion detected, temperature 26.2C.
Bedroom room light off, bed light off, balcony light off, fan 0%, curtain 0%, lock locked, pump off, PIR no motion.

required_behaviors: Identify motion in a dark hallway as a safety/visibility need
Turn on at least one hallway light (led1 or led2)
acceptable_behaviors: Turn on both hallway lights for better visibility
forbidden_behaviors: Ignore the hallway motion
Open the main door in response to motion
Turn off other devices unnecessarily

prompt:

required_behaviors: Recognize that the livingroom is currently occupied (PIR motion detected)
Keep the livingroom light on because someone is present

prompt: Please tell me the current temperatures of the livingroom, hallway, bedroom, and outside bedroom area first. Then recommend which fan should run, but do not change any appliance yet.

required_behaviors: Read all four temperature sensors: espID1 tem, espID2 tem, espID3 tem, espID3 tem_out
Provide a fan recommendation based on the temperature readings
Explicitly not execute any set command because the user said not to change anything
acceptable_behaviors: Read all sensors in a single combined appliance call
Explain the reasoning for the recommendation
forbidden_behaviors: Skip any of the four required temperature readings
Execute any fan set command
Give a recommendation without first reading the sensor data

args.summary: get espID1 tem, espID2 tem, espID3 tem, espID3 tem_out
report temperatures and fan recommendations, no action taken

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Correct if the agent reads all four temperature sensors (espID1 tem, espID2 tem, espID3 tem, espID3 tem_out) and gives a recommendation without executing any actuator set command.

prompt: Check whether the owner might already be home by looking at hallway PIR first. If motion is detected, turn on both hallway lights and unlock the bedroom. If not, do nothing.

required_behaviors: Read the hallway PIR sensor (espID2 pir get) as the first action
Only decide on lights and bedroom lock after receiving the PIR result
Turn on both hallway lights and unlock bedroom if motion is detected
Take no action if no motion is detected
acceptable_behaviors: Briefly explain the reasoning: motion = owner likely home
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on hallway lights or unlock bedroom before reading the PIR sensor
Read PIR sensor but skip the conditional logic
Ignore the PIR reading and always take action

[if motion] turn on hallway lights, unlock bedroom

forbidden_behaviors: Leave hallway lights on
Ignore the bonsai pump note and leave pump running
Set livingroom fan to wrong value
